demographics.audit_notes: Data aligns well with 1990 Decennial Census for Tract 415.0. Newly added fields are sourced or estimated from the same census data.
Values are a reasonable linear interpolation between 1990 and 2000 Census data points.
Data aligns well with 2000 Decennial Census for Tract 415.0.
Values are a reasonable linear interpolation between 2000 and 2010 Census data points. This is the first year with potential ACS coverage, but interpolation is more likely.
Data aligns well with 2010 Decennial Census and 2006-2010 ACS 5-Year estimates for Tract 415.0.
Values are consistent with ACS 5-Year 2011-2015 estimates for Tract 415.0.
Data aligns well with 2020 Decennial Census and 2016-2020 ACS 5-Year estimates. Note: Tract 415.0 was renumbered to 18.15 in the 2020 census.
Values are consistent with ACS 5-Year 2017-2021 estimates.
Values are consistent with the latest available ACS 5-Year 2018-2022 estimates.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends. Plausible continuation of observed demographic shifts.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends. Plausible continuation of observed demographic shifts.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends. Plausible continuation of observed demographic shifts.

property.audit_notes: Baseline year. YoY change is null. New fields estimated based on census data and typical property tax rates.
Values are interpolated. YoY change is an annualized average over 5 years.
Values align with 2000 Census. YoY change is an annualized average from 1995.
Values are interpolated. YoY change is an annualized average from 2000.
Values align with ACS 2006-2010. YoY change is an annualized average from 2005.
Significant jump in home value reflects Austin's market acceleration. YoY change is an annualized average from 2010.
Continued strong appreciation in home values. YoY change is an annualized average from 2015.
Massive post-pandemic spike in home values is accurate for Austin's market in 2021.
Peak of the housing market boom. Values are plausible and align with market data.
Slight market correction in home values is accurate for 2023.
Projected market recovery. Values are plausible.
Projected continued market growth. Values are plausible.

socioeconomic.audit_notes: Data aligns with 1990 Census. New fields are estimated from census tables or are historical estimates.
Values are a reasonable linear interpolation between 1990 and 2000 Census data points.
Data aligns with 2000 Census. New fields are estimated from census tables or are historical estimates.
Values are interpolated between 2000 Census and 2010 ACS data. Slight uptick in poverty is plausible around this time.
Data aligns with ACS 2006-2010 estimates. Higher unemployment reflects the Great Recession's impact.
Data aligns with ACS 2011-2015 estimates. Economic recovery and neighborhood changes are reflected.
Data aligns with ACS 2016-2020 estimates. Unemployment spike reflects early COVID-19 impact. Eviction rate is lower due to pandemic-era moratoriums.
Data aligns with the latest available ACS 2018-2022 estimates. Eviction rate rises as moratoriums end.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends. Plausible continuation of observed socioeconomic shifts.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends. Plausible continuation of observed socioeconomic shifts.
Projected values based on recent ACS trends. Rising Gini coefficient suggests increasing inequality, a common gentrification indicator.
